properties.remark: PUBLIC REPORTED PEA TO GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL COVERING THE GROUND.

Highest wind lasted about 10 minutes. Extensive damage included: 5 empty anchored down hopper style grain bins blown over, trailer rolled through the yard, gazebo destroyed, pool fence blown down and tall cottonwood trees snapped. Farm had wind meter that recorded 110 mph 823 pm to 824 pm. Damage 1/4 mile west of this 6 N Balta location included trees uprooted. 2 miles west of Balta and 1 mile south, near Davis Lake, had evergreen trees, around a home, sheared off at roof level. Damage continued through Rugby with a lot of large tree branches broken off there, and a few trees snapped.
Multiple downed tree branches reported on social media.
Numerous trees damaged. Cottonwoods (soft wood) 12 to 18 inches in diameter were snapped. Poplar branches (hard wood) 6 to 10 inches in diameter were also snapped. Report relayed via social media.
DAVIS private weather instrument observation.
Relayed via social media.
Mesonet station 327269 3 WSW Rugby (NDAWN).
Very gust winds around 60 mph and hail stones around quarter size to half dollar size.
Delayed report, relayed via social media and estimated time based on Radar.

Time estimated based on radar and NDDOT wind gust observation from near Dunseith. Many large tree branches down. Several trees were uprooted. Some damage to roofs. Power lines down. In one case a tree fell onto a powerline then onto a house, causing the house to start on fire. In another case a teenager (exact age unknown) was injured (indirect - after the fact) as he was burned while cleaning up tree damage and unknowingly came in contact with a live downed electrical wire. The teen was hospitalized. NDDOT wind gust measured 58 mph 1 mile SE of Dunseith. The damage in the city, away from the wind recorder, would indicate that wind speeds in the city were higher, on the order of 90 mph (preliminary estimate).
ND DOT station US-281 near Dunseith.

Time estimated based on radar are other observations from the area. Part of a porch was torn from a home. Several Tepees were blown away. Wind estimated at 60 mph. Based on this wind being out ahead of the thunderstorm and likely on the gust front of the storm complex, and more importantly a video of the event, this was most likely a gustnado, a small whirlwind that forms as an eddy in a thunderstorms outflow. Gustnadoes do not connect to the storm base and so are not considered tornadoes. The video clearly shows what looks similar to a dust devil with cloud base connection lacking. The NWS records gustnadoes as Thunderstorm Wind events and not tornadoes.
pea size hail and heavy rain. Ongoing at the time of the call.
Unknown hail size. Heavy rain.

Delayed report. Relayed via social media with photos of the damage. A door was blown off of a quonset and about a quarter of the tin from the roof was also blown away. A horse trailer was blown out into a field, about 100 yards from where it was parked.
Damage to the roof of an outbuilding on a farm. An unattached horse trailer was pushed through a field. Property had an anemometer and measured 62 mph at which point the cups broke off.
Large tree branches were broken.

*** 1 INJ *** A manufactured home was destroyed along with a number of out buildings and 10 telephone poles, 6 miles east southeast of Selfridge in Sioux County ND. The approximate time based on radar was 10:18 through 10:25 PM CDT. One injury was reported.

*** 1 INJ *** Damage survey revealed that a microburst with wind speeds around 125 mph struck this property and completely destroyed a manufactured home which rolled onto a vehicle crushing the roof and partially damaging another vehicle. Parts of the home were blown into a barn, severely damaging it. The debris pattern was divergent in nature and oriented toward the E/SE. Two people were in the home at the time. One was injured and hospitalized.

Reports a lot of tree damage in the town of Steele. About three dozen trees total had some kind of damage. Some were snapped in half, some were uprooted, some had branches broken off of 6 to 18 inches in diameter. One tree branch was blocking a road. Duration was 5 minutes. Power is also out in town. Estimated wind speed of at least 70 MPH. Maximum hail size was a nickel.
